title: Function calling, tool use, MCP + DSA Greedy finish
focus: Schemas, retries, hallucinated args; MCP architecture.

items.label: Partition Labels
interviewQuestions: Prove the greedy choice — why is the locally-optimal pick safe globally? (Exchange argument or staying-ahead.)
When does greedy fail on a similar-looking problem, and what would you reach for instead (DP, BFS)?
Walk through edge cases that often break naive greedy: ties, negatives, single element.

items.label: Valid Parenthesis String
interviewQuestions: Prove the greedy choice — why is the locally-optimal pick safe globally? (Exchange argument or staying-ahead.)
When does greedy fail on a similar-looking problem, and what would you reach for instead (DP, BFS)?
Walk through edge cases that often break naive greedy: ties, negatives, single element.

items.label: Function calling: schemas, parsing, retries
interviewQuestions: Walk through how function calling works under the hood — what does the model actually emit?
How do you handle a model that hallucinates a tool call with bad arguments?

items.label: Designing tool catalogs for agents
interviewQuestions: How would you design a tool catalog for a research agent — what are the trade-offs in granularity?
Why do agents perform worse when you give them too many tools, and what's a good cutoff?

items.label: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
interviewQuestions: What problem does MCP solve compared to ad-hoc function calling?
Walk me through how an MCP server is built and what guarantees the protocol gives you.
